title: Bring up LilyGO GNSS, time, battery, and power services
description: Why this exists: location, time, battery, and power behavior are common sources of board-specific leakage. What needs to be done: validate GNSS UART/I2C if present, PPS/time source if present, battery ADC or PMIC telemetry, charging status, power-hold/shutdown controls, wake buttons, and any external flash/storage. Integrate through firmware-wide location/date-time providers and HAL capability discovery rather than board-specific app logic. Acceptance criteria: each supported service reports present/unavailable correctly; GNSS/time feed into provider abstractions where available; battery/power state appears in status resources/UI; unsupported or uncertain services have follow-up Beads.
notes:
Design input from user on 2026-07-02: consider embedding build-time timestamps in firmware and/or board metadata as an epoch hint for GNSS/time bring-up. Treat as a lower-bound/warm-start source for time validity checks and stale-fix rejection, not as authoritative wall-clock time; no-hardware design/tests can cover build-epoch plumbing, while real GNSS lock validation remains hardware-blocked.
2026-07-02 no-hardware implementation evidence:
- Implemented the remaining software bridge for the user build-epoch/GNSS idea: Zephyr GNSS UTC from puck_location now submits a LICHEN_HAL_TIME_SOURCE_GNSS sample into the HAL time provider. The existing HAL build/provision epoch floor decides whether that GNSS time can establish wall clock; location submission still succeeds when time is rejected.
- Safety details: GNSS time is submitted only when fix_status is not GNSS_FIX_STATUS_NO_FIX, preventing no-fix publications with stale UTC fields from refreshing wall clock. GNSS UTC conversion validates month/day including leap years before timeutil_timegm64() so impossible dates are ignored instead of normalized.
- Tests added in lichen/tests/puck_location/src/main.c: no UTC does not establish wall clock; below-build-epoch UTC records BELOW_EPOCH_FLOOR while location submit returns success; impossible date (2024-02-31) is ignored; valid GNSS UTC establishes wall clock with age and quality; valid fix followed by no-fix carrying prior UTC does not refresh the HAL time sample.
- Existing no-hardware coverage confirmed: lichen/cmake/lichen_build_epoch.cmake, HAL Kconfig, HAL time provider, build_epoch tests, HAL epoch/provision tests, app-interface and gateway status propagation already cover build/provision epoch plumbing.
- Hardware follow-ups created: project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.8.1 for physical LilyGO GNSS/time epoch behavior; project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.8.2 for physical LilyGO battery/PMIC/power telemetry.
- Local check: git diff --check passed.
- Code review: Helmholtz found P1 stale/no-fix UTC refresh risk; fixed by requiring active fix and adding regression; re-review no P0/P1/P2. Jason found P2 impossible-date normalization risk; fixed by calendar validation and Feb 31 regression; re-review no P0/P1/P2.
- EC2 validation: instance i-05de3bc92a8e419aa in us-east-2a, EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr, clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-h7t5-7-8-validate from origin/main plus local patch. bootstrap-host hit known Ubuntu Noble awscli package gap; installed Zephyr host deps directly; Zephyr v3.7.0, west v1.5.0.
- Twister command: west twister -T lichen/tests/puck_location -T lichen/tests/hal -T lichen/tests/build_epoch -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/gateway_status -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out-h7t5-7-8 --inline-logs --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen
- Twister result: 12 scenarios / 24 instances selected; 21 passed, 0 failed, 0 errored, 3 skipped by static filter; 246 test cases executed; 106 skipped; 0 warnings; duration 509.80s. Passed key instances: app.puck.location_provider native_sim/qemu_x86 9 cases each; build_epoch source_date/release/developer_generated/developer_fixed native_sim/qemu_x86 1 case each; HAL native_defaults native_sim/qemu_x86 22 cases each, loopback_lora native_sim 22 cases, absent_power_qemu qemu_x86 22 cases, battery_adc native_sim/qemu_x86 22 cases each, power_providers native_sim/qemu_x86 23 cases each; app_interface native_sim 24 cases; gateway_status native_sim/qemu_x86 9 cases each.
- Representative size output: app.puck.location_provider native_sim text=44715 data=373 bss=13872 dec=58960; qemu_x86 text=78760 data=3176 bss=88836 dec=170772. Full size report was collected from twister-out-h7t5-7-8.
- EC2 cleanup: sync completed; /mnt/lichen-zephyr unmounted; vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 detached and available; instance i-05de3bc92a8e419aa terminated; temporary security group sg-0c5d5bba4688cb416 deleted.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware scope: GNSS UTC now feeds HAL time provider with build/provision epoch floor validation, native_sim/qemu tests passed, and physical GNSS/time plus battery/PMIC/power validation split to child Beads project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.8.1 and project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.8.2.
